    CMEMS SSS products — SMOS L3 (asc/des) and MULTIOBS L4¶

    Sea-surface salinity is the family with the least gridded depth in CMEMS: no per-mission gridded products other than SMOS (the dedicated salinity satellite) split by orbit direction, and a single multi-mission L4 derived from SMOS + SMAP + Argo. The notebook walks the SMOS asc/des → MULTIOBS L4 path so you can see how much smoothing the operational mapping does.

    Region & window: Gulf Stream [-70°, -50°] × [30°, 45°], 2026-01-01 → 2026-01-15 (same as the SSH notebook for cross-family consistency).

    Credentials live in the project-root .env (COPERNICUSMARINE_SERVICE_USERNAME / ..._PASSWORD).

    1. Setup¶

    In [1]:
    Copied! 
    from __future__ import annotations

import warnings

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import xarray as xr
from dotenv import load_dotenv

import copernicusmarine as cm

from xrtoolz.interpolate import Grid

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=FutureWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=RuntimeWarning)
load_dotenv()
    from __future__ import annotations import warnings import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import numpy as np import xarray as xr from dotenv import load_dotenv import copernicusmarine as cm from xrtoolz.interpolate import Grid warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=FutureWarning) warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=RuntimeWarning) load_dotenv()

    In [2]:
    Copied! 
    BBOX = dict(
    minimum_longitude=-70.0, maximum_longitude=-50.0,
    minimum_latitude=30.0,   maximum_latitude=45.0,
)
TIME = dict(start_datetime='2026-01-01', end_datetime='2026-01-15')

# Common 0.25° reference grid for cross-product comparison.
GRID = Grid.from_bounds(
    lon_bnds=(BBOX['minimum_longitude'], BBOX['maximum_longitude']),
    lat_bnds=(BBOX['minimum_latitude'],  BBOX['maximum_latitude']),
    resolution=0.25,
)
ref = xr.Dataset(coords={'longitude': GRID.lon, 'latitude': GRID.lat})
GRID.lon.shape, GRID.lat.shape
    BBOX = dict( minimum_longitude=-70.0, maximum_longitude=-50.0, minimum_latitude=30.0, maximum_latitude=45.0, ) TIME = dict(start_datetime='2026-01-01', end_datetime='2026-01-15') # Common 0.25° reference grid for cross-product comparison. GRID = Grid.from_bounds( lon_bnds=(BBOX['minimum_longitude'], BBOX['maximum_longitude']), lat_bnds=(BBOX['minimum_latitude'], BBOX['maximum_latitude']), resolution=0.25, ) ref = xr.Dataset(coords={'longitude': GRID.lon, 'latitude': GRID.lat}) GRID.lon.shape, GRID.lat.shape
    Out[2]:
    ((81,), (61,))
    In [3]:
    Copied! 
    def to_common_grid(da: xr.DataArray, *, ref: xr.Dataset = ref) -> xr.DataArray:
    """Interpolate a 2D DataArray (with lat/lon coords) onto the 0.25° reference grid.

    Source resolutions in this family vary (0.05°, 0.1°, 0.25°, 4 km, 300 m); we put
    everything on a single 0.25° grid so cross-product panels share a coordinate system.
    """
    return da.interp(
        latitude=ref['latitude'],
        longitude=ref['longitude'],
        method='linear',
    )
    def to_common_grid(da: xr.DataArray, *, ref: xr.Dataset = ref) -> xr.DataArray: """Interpolate a 2D DataArray (with lat/lon coords) onto the 0.25° reference grid. Source resolutions in this family vary (0.05°, 0.1°, 0.25°, 4 km, 300 m); we put everything on a single 0.25° grid so cross-product panels share a coordinate system. """ return da.interp( latitude=ref['latitude'], longitude=ref['longitude'], method='linear', )

    2. SMOS L3 — ascending vs descending¶

    SMOS flies a sun-synchronous orbit; ascending and descending passes hit the same scene at different local times of day. CMEMS publishes the two as separate L3 products at 0.25°. Both are NaN-heavy daily fields — swath edges, RFI flagging, ice.

    In [4]:
    Copied! 
    L3_SMOS = {
    'asc': 'cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_mynrt_smos-asc_P1D',
    'des': 'cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_mynrt_smos-des_P1D',
}
ds_smos = {
    label: cm.open_dataset(dataset_id=did, **BBOX, **TIME)
    for label, did in L3_SMOS.items()
}
ds_smos['asc']
    L3_SMOS = { 'asc': 'cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_mynrt_smos-asc_P1D', 'des': 'cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_mynrt_smos-des_P1D', } ds_smos = { label: cm.open_dataset(dataset_id=did, **BBOX, **TIME) for label, did in L3_SMOS.items() } ds_smos['asc']

    In [5]:
    Copied! 
    DAY = '2026-01-08'
vmin, vmax = 33.5, 37.5  # PSU
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4))
for ax, (label, ds) in zip(axes, ds_smos.items(), strict=False):
    sss = ds['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze()
    sss.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax,
                        cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'})
    cov = float(np.isfinite(sss).mean())
    ax.set_title(f'SMOS {label} on {DAY} — coverage: {cov:.0%}')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    DAY = '2026-01-08' vmin, vmax = 33.5, 37.5 # PSU fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4)) for ax, (label, ds) in zip(axes, ds_smos.items(), strict=False): sss = ds['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze() sss.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'}) cov = float(np.isfinite(sss).mean()) ax.set_title(f'SMOS {label} on {DAY} — coverage: {cov:.0%}') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    15-day SMOS asc + des composites¶

    Means across the window — the per-orbit bias is small in this region but visible at the edges of swath cusps.

    In [6]:
    Copied! 
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4))
for ax, (label, ds) in zip(axes, ds_smos.items(), strict=False):
    ds['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time').plot.pcolormesh(
        ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax,
        cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'},
    )
    ax.set_title(f'SMOS {label} — 15-day mean')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4)) for ax, (label, ds) in zip(axes, ds_smos.items(), strict=False): ds['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time').plot.pcolormesh( ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'}, ) ax.set_title(f'SMOS {label} — 15-day mean') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    3. MULTIOBS L4 — daily multi-mission analysis¶

    Brings SMOS asc + des, SMAP, and Argo float surface SSS into a single 0.125° daily field with surface density (sos, dos).

    In [7]:
    Copied! 
    ds_l4 = cm.open_dataset(
    dataset_id='cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_nrt_multi_P1D',
    **BBOX, **TIME,
)
list(ds_l4.data_vars), dict(ds_l4.sizes)
    ds_l4 = cm.open_dataset( dataset_id='cmems_obs-mob_glo_phy-sss_nrt_multi_P1D', **BBOX, **TIME, ) list(ds_l4.data_vars), dict(ds_l4.sizes)

  np.str_('dos_error'),
  np.str_('sea_ice_fraction'),
  np.str_('sos'),

 {'time': 15, 'depth': 1, 'latitude': 120, 'longitude': 160})
    In [8]:
    Copied! 
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4))
ds_l4['sos'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze().plot.pcolormesh(
    ax=axes[0], cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax,
    cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'},
)
axes[0].set_title(f'MULTIOBS L4 SSS on {DAY}')
ds_l4['dos'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze().plot.pcolormesh(
    ax=axes[1], cmap='cividis',
    cbar_kwargs={'label': 'σ (kg/m³)'},
)
axes[1].set_title(f'MULTIOBS L4 surface density on {DAY}')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.4)) ds_l4['sos'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze().plot.pcolormesh( ax=axes[0], cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, cbar_kwargs={'label': 'SSS (PSU)'}, ) axes[0].set_title(f'MULTIOBS L4 SSS on {DAY}') ds_l4['dos'].sel(time=DAY, method='nearest').squeeze().plot.pcolormesh( ax=axes[1], cmap='cividis', cbar_kwargs={'label': 'σ (kg/m³)'}, ) axes[1].set_title(f'MULTIOBS L4 surface density on {DAY}') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    4. Cross-product comparison at 0.25°¶

    Three products on the common reference grid: SMOS asc / SMOS des / MULTIOBS L4. The L4 will look much smoother — that's OI doing its job.

    In [9]:
    Copied! 
    products_25 = {
    'SMOS asc': to_common_grid(ds_smos['asc']['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time')),
    'SMOS des': to_common_grid(ds_smos['des']['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time')),
    'MULTIOBS L4': to_common_grid(ds_l4['sos'].mean('time').squeeze()),
}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4.4), sharex=True, sharey=True)
for ax, (label, da) in zip(axes, products_25.items(), strict=False):
    da.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax,
                       add_colorbar=False)
    cov = float(np.isfinite(da).mean())
    ax.set_title(f'{label}\ncoverage: {cov:.0%}')
fig.suptitle('15-day mean SSS — every product on the common 0.25° grid', y=1.04)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    products_25 = { 'SMOS asc': to_common_grid(ds_smos['asc']['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time')), 'SMOS des': to_common_grid(ds_smos['des']['Sea_Surface_Salinity'].mean('time')), 'MULTIOBS L4': to_common_grid(ds_l4['sos'].mean('time').squeeze()), } fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4.4), sharex=True, sharey=True) for ax, (label, da) in zip(axes, products_25.items(), strict=False): da.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='viridis', vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, add_colorbar=False) cov = float(np.isfinite(da).mean()) ax.set_title(f'{label}\ncoverage: {cov:.0%}') fig.suptitle('15-day mean SSS — every product on the common 0.25° grid', y=1.04) plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    MULTIOBS minus SMOS-merged¶

    Take the simple mean of the two SMOS streams as a poor-man's L3-merge and difference against the operational L4.

    In [10]:
    Copied! 
    smos_merge = (products_25['SMOS asc'] + products_25['SMOS des']) / 2
diff = products_25['MULTIOBS L4'] - smos_merge
dvmax = float(np.nanpercentile(np.abs(diff.values), 95)) or 0.5
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4.5))
diff.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='RdBu_r', vmin=-dvmax, vmax=dvmax,
                     cbar_kwargs={'label': 'Δ SSS (PSU)'})
ax.set_title('MULTIOBS L4 − ½(SMOS asc + des), 15-day mean')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    smos_merge = (products_25['SMOS asc'] + products_25['SMOS des']) / 2 diff = products_25['MULTIOBS L4'] - smos_merge dvmax = float(np.nanpercentile(np.abs(diff.values), 95)) or 0.5 fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4.5)) diff.plot.pcolormesh(ax=ax, cmap='RdBu_r', vmin=-dvmax, vmax=dvmax, cbar_kwargs={'label': 'Δ SSS (PSU)'}) ax.set_title('MULTIOBS L4 − ½(SMOS asc + des), 15-day mean') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    Takeaways¶

    • SMOS is the only single-mission gridded SSS product on CMEMS — split into asc / des because the two have measurably different bias structure.
    • MULTIOBS L4 is the only operational gap-free SSS field. Its smoothness comes from OI, not from a denser observation set: the satellite SSS constellation is genuinely sparse compared with SST/OC.
    • For physical SSS gradients (Gulf Stream front, river plumes), L4 is the right product. For algorithm-development or DA where you want the actual obs uncertainty, you want SMOS L3 + SMAP L3 (the latter not on CMEMS — see issue #20).
    Made with Material for MkDocs
